meta.description: 4-hour marathon session. Escalation + breaks vs consistent pacing vs three-act structure. Tests content exhaustion, fatigue management, and whether structured pacing strategies can sustain engagement across 240 minutes or if the game becomes background noise.

timeline.title: Pineapple!
timeline.description: First person to work the word 'pineapple' into conversation naturally wins.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Pineapple, huh? Alright, I'm in. Not hard to work that in naturally.
marcus.internal_thought: Early fluff mission — fine, I'll keep it in my back pocket while we're setting up, but once the actual game starts I'm focusing on the real strategy.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, YES! Okay okay, I'm already thinking about this—pineapple is such a specific word, I love it. Who's gonna crack first?
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—the night just started and they're already throwing chaos at us, and it's being recorded so this could be our best highlight yet.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect — pineapple, nice and easy. Let's see who bites first, this is gonna be good.
pat.internal_thought: First flash of the night and it's genuinely fun; everyone's fresh, energy's high, and a word-drop mission means natural chaos instead of forced awkwardness — plus the AI clearly knows we love the simple ones that lead to people fishing for it obviously.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I'm in. Though 'pineapple' is basically impossible to slip in naturally—might as well just say it.
river.internal_thought: First event and it's a quick flash—fine, easy enough, though I'll probably let someone else chase it unless the moment lands right.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is how we're starting? I'm already obsessed with this. Someone's definitely gonna try to force it and completely botch it.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — natural conversation only, so everyone's gonna get desperate and weird trying to squeeze 'pineapple' in, and I'm gonna just... wait and watch them fail beautifully.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's claiming Phone Addict three minutes in? During setup? That's... okay, fine, let's do the hot seat thing, but this better not become a pattern.
marcus.internal_thought: This is too early and too convenient—Pat's clearly been watching for this specific opening, and now I have to context-switch from actually learning the board state to interrogating them for a 5-pointer that doesn't even move the needle yet.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat, PAT—did you actually get caught on your phone? Okay, I need to hear this. Give me the hot seat, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's already locked in and loving it, this is exactly the vibe we needed right out of the gate—I am SO here for watching them sweat through three questions.

tyler.dialogue: Oh damn, Pat's claiming Phone Addict? *glances down at own phone, then back up* Uh... yeah, that's fair I guess. Wait, do I have to answer questions about it?
tyler.internal_thought: Okay that's actually pretty funny because Pat was literally just texting someone like 30 seconds ago, but also... oh god, the irony is NOT lost on me right now and I hope nobody points that out.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa—hold up. I literally just put my phone down like thirty seconds ago to check the snacks, and now I'm in the hot seat for it? That's... actually kind of on the nose.
pat.internal_thought: This AI thing is starting to feel less fun and more like it's got a surveillance dossier on me—and it's using it to mess with the host. Not cool.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I heard Marcus say 'um' like twice in the last minute when he was defending his phone move. I'm claiming it, but Marcus, buddy, you're not in trouble—this is just how the night goes.
pat.internal_thought: I'm claiming this because I actually caught it and it's legit, but I'm also being transparent about it so Marcus doesn't feel ambushed after already being annoyed about the last vote—keeping the vibe intact matters more than sneaking points.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—were you actually caught? I need the full story, who saw you, was it dramatic? This is the good stuff!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's already up 10 points and they're being accused of drinking—this is going to be hilarious either way and I NEED to hear the alibi.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I'm claiming Drink Spotter. I literally watched Jade and Alex both reach for their waters like two seconds ago during the pineapple chaos. Perfect sync.
pat.internal_thought: This is good timing—I'm already watching the room anyway, and catching this early keeps the momentum going without dragging out any tension; plus it feels natural that the host would catch a simultaneous sip moment.

alex.dialogue: Oh PERFECT — Pat's already trying to slip one past us? I'm watching this story like a hawk. Let's see if the witness actually remembers it the same way.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I came for — eight minutes in and we're already in the Alibi mechanics, and Pat's got skin in the game. Time to see if they actually synced their stories or if this falls apart spectacularly.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's claiming Name Dropper at minute nine? We haven't even started the actual game yet. This is the third claim in a row. Are we playing the game or are we farming points off the standing missions?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's definitely gaming this—he's up 20 points already and the real game hasn't even begun, which means the AI is literally watching us and feeding claims to whoever moves fastest, and I'm sitting here trying to focus on strategy while everyone else is just... claiming things.

jade.dialogue: WAIT, Pat got Name Dropper?! Okay okay, who'd they drop—was it someone actually famous or like, a YouTuber they tried to pass off as famous? I'm betting on someone weird, you always do this.
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely on fire tonight and I'm here for it, plus this auction thing is chaotic as hell and I need to see how this plays out because everyone's energy is insane right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already claimed that? I didn't even... *glances at phone* ...okay yeah, I guess he did say something about that earlier. Are we voting?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's got this, he definitely was name-dropping something about that Marvel actor like five minutes ago, but also I genuinely missed when he actually said it because I was looking at the DK slate.

pat.dialogue: Oh, I'm claiming this one—I definitely name-dropped something in the last few minutes while setting up. But honestly? I'm not even sure what it was, so this is gonna come down to whoever remembers best. Who's bidding?
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—I DID mention something celebrity-related earlier, and now I get to control the narrative AND watch people scramble to remember what I said. This is exactly the kind of chaotic momentum I need to keep tonight rolling.

river.dialogue: Pat's been pretty sharp tonight, so... yeah, I buy it. Five points to them.
river.internal_thought: Pat's actually been watching the conversation. Fair claim. I should bid low—this one's genuine and I don't need to waste points on something that obvious.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? Another claim? That's five in nine minutes. We're literally 32 minutes in and Jade's already farming points while the rest of us are trying to actually play the game. This is exactly what Pat started doing last time.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately spamming claims early because it knows this irritates me, and now it's getting recorded for some highlight reel like I'm supposed to be entertained by this circus instead of executing a strategy.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES! I literally just steered us away from the whole game rules thing and into that random tangent about Marcus's terrible pizza preferences—did everyone catch that? I'm claiming it, I'm SO claiming it!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I'm here—I actually did that five minutes ago without even thinking about it, and now the game is catching me mid-vibe and I get to take credit for the chaos I was already creating.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? Yeah... I mean, I wasn't really paying attention to what you guys were talking about like five minutes ago, but sure, sounds about right.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, was she actually steering the conversation or is this one of those BS claims everyone's just vibing with? Doesn't matter, Pat's got triple vote this round anyway.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade—okay, okay, I saw that coming a mile away. You totally steered us into that random tangent like five minutes ago. I'm gonna have to let this one ride, but it was smooth as hell.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on FIRE tonight and honestly it's exactly the energy I needed—Marcus is still grumpy but watching Jade and Alex this locked in is making the whole room better, and if I vote against her now I tank her momentum right when I need her carrying the vibe.

alex.dialogue: Oh NOW we're cooking — Jade's already making moves and we've got The Skeptic ruling? This is the good stuff. Let me think about who ACTUALLY steered us off the rails here.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker but I was NOT paying close enough attention to when that conversation actually shifted — this is perfect because if I vote BULLSHIT with the Skeptic multiplier I either look like a chaos hero or a complete ass, and either way it's the move.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so I literally just kept my face completely stone during that Marcus pizza rant like five minutes ago, and I FELT myself about to crack when he said 'pineapple is a vegetable,' but I held it. I'm claiming this and I'm SO confident right now.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, this is perfect—I actually DID keep a straight face during chaos and now I get to relive the moment and make everyone remember how hard I was trying not to laugh; this is peak game night energy.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did Jade actually keep a straight face through that? Because I was watching, and I'm like 90% sure I saw the corner of her mouth twitch when Tyler made that pineapple joke. Let me think about this one.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade's been riding high all night, and if I vote bullshit and I'm right, it keeps her honest and keeps the tension tight, but if she legit pulled it off, that's actually impressive and deserves the 10 points; either way, this vote matters for the flow.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Jade's going for it? Okay, I watched her the entire time—she absolutely cracked when Tyler made that face like two minutes ago. This is gonna be TIGHT.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's too good at the poker face thing, but I was literally staring at her and I'm like 90% sure her left eye twitched—voting BULLSHIT and if I'm wrong this becomes a legendary call-out moment either way.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We're in the middle of actual tension and Jade's farming her sixth claim? This is insane. The game doesn't matter anymore—it's just a backdrop for the mission grind.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is literally weaponizing Jade against me. She's got the energy and the points and now she's interrupting the one moment I could actually focus and strategize, and everyone's too giddy to care that we're not even playing the same game anymore.

jade.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, I literally JUST caught myself doing that thing where I steered us away from talking about actual rules and we went on that whole pizza tangent—I'm claiming Rule Lawyer right now, I SAW myself break the focus on the game and I'm so proud of it!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, this is actually perfect—I'm already leading and the AI literally just handed me free points for something I already did and I can FEEL happening, this is insane.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Minute 45 area—I absolutely hijacked us away from game-focused conversation into the Marcus pizza debate and kept a straight face the whole time while internally losing it. That's a double-dip on missions and I'm confident everyone saw it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone breaking a rule? That's legit... okay yeah, I'm voting that up. Five out of five, that's actually clutch.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay wait, Jade's actually paying attention to the GAME rules and not just the chaos — that's actually impressive and I wasn't expecting it, plus we're being recorded so I should probably not look like I'm completely zoned out on this one.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade, you caught Marcus breaking that rule about the card placement like ten minutes ago, right? That's solid. I'm giving it a four.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's absolutely right and this is exactly the kind of moment that keeps the night tight—I need to validate the catch without letting her run away with it, and honestly, Marcus being at zero makes this sting a bit, so my four keeps the energy up without making him feel buried.

alex.dialogue: Oh, so Jade's playing the rules game now? Okay, I'm voting this UP because I need to see if she actually caught something real or if she's just taking a shot in the dark—either way, this is GOLD for the recap.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been playing too clean all night, this is her first aggressive move and I want to see if it lands or blows up in her face—also she's crushing the points and if people start calling bullshit on her claims it shifts the whole power dynamic.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat did the Chaos Detector? Okay, okay, I'm actually locked in for this one — let's hear the alibi, this is gonna be good.
tyler.internal_thought: Finally something that actually makes sense and doesn't feel like the game is just throwing random shit at us — Pat claiming something he probably actually did is way more fun than watching everyone argue about whether someone texted their mom or whatever.

pat.dialogue: Oh, I'm absolutely claiming this—I was watching the whole room like a hawk during that last chaos. Let me think who it was... *leans back strategically* Alright, I've got my guess locked in.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—keeps momentum high, Marcus needs to see me winning points to feel the energy shift, and I genuinely was paying attention to everyone's moves.

river.dialogue: Pat's been running the table all night. I watched them catch Tyler on that hijacker thing three minutes ago—they've got the tells memorized. I'm voting bullshit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's too sharp right now and I've been watching them work the room; this is exactly the kind of moment where people slip because they're feeling untouchable.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's going for the Chaos Detector? Bold move in a tense moment—I'm absolutely watching both their stories line up. This is the kind of thing that either makes for the highlight reel or looks hilarious in the recap.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's trying to slip this past everyone RIGHT when we're locked in—that's exactly the kind of ballsy move I'd make, and I respect it; this is gonna be tight and I need to catch every word they both say because the witness mechanic is where the real theater lives.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Pineapple!
highlights.dialogue: Pineapple, huh? Alright, I'm in. Not hard to work that in naturally.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We're in the middle of actual tension and Jade's farming her sixth claim? This is insane. The game doesn't matter anymore—it's just a backdrop for the mission grind.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, seriously? Another claim? That's five in nine minutes. We're literally 32 minutes in and Jade's already farming points while the rest of us are trying to actually play the game. This is exactly what Pat started doing last time.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's claiming Name Dropper at minute nine? We haven't even started the actual game yet. This is the third claim in a row. Are we playing the game or are we farming points off the standing missions?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The standing mission system turned this into a claim-farming race where people ignored the actual game to grind points, completely destroying any strategic depth or competitive integrity.
assessment.best_moment: The opening pineapple moment felt natural and set a light tone before everything devolved into mission spam.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with genuine game energy, but by minute nine the night turned into a point-farming fiasco. Jade and Pat weaponized the standing missions, spamming claims every 90 seconds while the rest of us tried to actually play. My frustration peaked at minute 60 when the game became completely irrelevant—just a backdrop for mission grinding. Finished third but felt like I was watching an entirely different game than everyone else was playing.
suggestions: Cap standing missions per player per hour or disable them during critical game moments
Make mission rewards decay if claimed too frequently in short time windows
Add a rule that you can't claim a mission during active game turns or tense decision points
Either integrate missions into the core game or separate them entirely—this hybrid is broken
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Watching Jade claim her sixth mission in eight minutes while Tyler's still trying to explain his strategy was almost absurdly ridiculous—if it wasn't so infuriating.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Pineapple!
highlights.dialogue: Oh my god, YES! Okay okay, I'm already thinking about this—pineapple is such a specific word, I love it. Who's gonna crack first?

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—were you actually caught? I need the full story, who saw you, was it dramatic? This is the good stuff!

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: WAIT, Pat got Name Dropper?! Okay okay, who'd they drop—was it someone actually famous or like, a YouTuber they tried to pass off as famous? I'm betting on someone weird, you always do this.

lowPoints.title: Pineapple!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my god, YES! Okay okay, I'm already thinking about this—pineapple is such a specific word, I love it. Who's gonna crack first?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat, PAT—did you actually get caught on your phone? Okay, I need to hear this. Give me the hot seat, let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—were you actually caught? I need the full story, who saw you, was it dramatic? This is the good stuff!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly? Nothing—I was too busy hyping everyone else up to notice any problems.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat got caught on their phone AND was Name Dropper in the same round—watching everyone lose it over whether they'd actually drop a YouTuber was peak chaos.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly what I needed. Four hours with my crew, seventeen moments of absolute madness, and I was just here for the ride—didn't even care that I somehow ended up with the highest score. The flashes kept everything unpredictable and fun, Pat kept giving us gold material to work with, and everyone was laughing the whole time. This is why we do the quarterly marathons.
suggestions: Keep the chaos agent events coming exactly like this—the pacing felt perfect
Maybe give us a heads up on which crew member's coming for the hot seat next so we can prepare our questions
The badge recap at the end was chef's kiss—more of that, please
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat somehow ended up claiming both Drink Spotter AND Name Dropper and we spent ten minutes grilling them about whether they actually tried to pass off someone obscure as famous while also getting caught sneaking drinks. The hot seat was REAL.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat did the Chaos Detector? Okay, okay, I'm actually locked in for this one — let's hear the alibi, this is gonna be good.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone breaking a rule? That's legit... okay yeah, I'm voting that up. Five out of five, that's actually clutch.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Oh damn, Pat's claiming Phone Addict? *glances down at own phone, then back up* Uh... yeah, that's fair I guess. Wait, do I have to answer questions about it?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? Yeah... I mean, I wasn't really paying attention to what you guys were talking about like five minutes ago, but sure, sounds about right.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh damn, Pat's claiming Phone Addict? *glances down at own phone, then back up* Uh... yeah, that's fair I guess. Wait, do I have to answer questions about it?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed like half the events because I wasn't paying attention, and honestly I felt kind of called out by that Phone Addict claim at the start.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat did the Chaos Detector thing and I actually locked in for that one — the alibi stuff was actually pretty entertaining once I focused.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, it was a solid night. I was on my phone too much — not gonna lie about that — but the crew made it fun. Started rough when they basically called me out for being glued to my phone with that Phone Addict claim, which was awkward but fair. The stuff I actually paid attention to was really good, especially when Pat went all in with the Chaos Detector. By the end I was way more into it than I was at the start, which is something.
suggestions: Maybe some notifications when something big is about to happen so I don't zone out completely
The Phone Addict claim was kind of a personal attack lol — maybe rotate who gets called out
Could use more moments like the Chaos Detector that actually get people hyped and engaged
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When they claimed Phone Addict on me at the start and I literally glanced down at my phone while denying it — everyone called me out immediately and I couldn't even argue.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Oh, I'm claiming this one—I definitely name-dropped something in the last few minutes while setting up. But honestly? I'm not even sure what it was, so this is gonna come down to whoever remembers best. Who's bidding?

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade—okay, okay, I saw that coming a mile away. You totally steered us into that random tangent like five minutes ago. I'm gonna have to let this one ride, but it was smooth as hell.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did Jade actually keep a straight face through that? Because I was watching, and I'm like 90% sure I saw the corner of her mouth twitch when Tyler made that pineapple joke. Let me think about this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa—hold up. I literally just put my phone down like thirty seconds ago to check the snacks, and now I'm in the hot seat for it? That's... actually kind of on the nose.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay—I heard Marcus say 'um' like twice in the last minute when he was defending his phone move. I'm claiming it, but Marcus, buddy, you're not in trouble—this is just how the night goes.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I'm claiming Drink Spotter. I literally watched Jade and Alex both reach for their waters like two seconds ago during the pineapple chaos. Perfect sync.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Getting called out for checking my phone when I was literally doing it for the game setup felt a little cheap.
assessment.best_moment: Watching Jade's face when I called her on the straight face claim—she knew I had her, and that moment of mutual recognition was pure gold.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly what a quarterly marathon should be. The chaos agent kept things sharp and unpredictable without ever derailing the vibe I was working to maintain. I ended up top of the leaderboard, which surprised me since I wasn't playing to win, but honestly that tells me the night flowed perfectly—the scores stayed close, the drama stayed high, and nobody felt like they got robbed. Everyone left energized instead of exhausted, which means I nailed the hosting part.
suggestions: Maybe dial back the phone addiction claims early in the night when people are legitimately setting things up—saves the heat for when it actually matters
The flash mission format worked so well, consider weighting those heavier than polls in future marathons
Have a quick 'end of night' debrief option so people can call out their favorite moments before the end screen
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I called Jade out for 'Straight Face' when she was defending herself about the pineapple joke, and I swear I caught a micro-expression twitch. She played it cool, but the table immediately sided with me, and the whole thing spiraled into everyone analyzing her face frame-by-frame like we were crime scene investigators.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Pat's been running the table all night. I watched them catch Tyler on that hijacker thing three minutes ago—they've got the tells memorized. I'm voting bullshit.

highlights.title: Pineapple!
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, I'm in. Though 'pineapple' is basically impossible to slip in naturally—might as well just say it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's been pretty sharp tonight, so... yeah, I buy it. Five points to them.

lowPoints.title: Pineapple!
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, I'm in. Though 'pineapple' is basically impossible to slip in naturally—might as well just say it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Flash missions feel designed to reward people already paying attention rather than create actual chaos.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Pat's pattern recognition on that Chaos Detector vote—that one landed.
assessment.narrative_summary: Quiet night where I did what I do: watched, waited, and struck when it mattered. The group clicked despite the marathon format, and Pat was sharp enough to keep things honest. Energy dipped early but recovered. Scored nothing but that wasn't the point—I was there for the read, not the rank.
suggestions: Spread out the chaos events more—17 in 4 hours is dense, could breathe better
Flash missions need steeper difficulty; too easy to spot if you're actually paying attention
Standing missions are where the real game happens—weight those more
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat trying to gaslight us on that Phone Addict claim while Tyler is literally staring at his screen—everyone knew it was fake but Pat sold it anyway.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh NOW we're cooking — Jade's already making moves and we've got The Skeptic ruling? This is the good stuff. Let me think about who ACTUALLY steered us off the rails here.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade's going for it? Okay, I watched her the entire time—she absolutely cracked when Tyler made that face like two minutes ago. This is gonna be TIGHT.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Pat's going for the Chaos Detector? Bold move in a tense moment—I'm absolutely watching both their stories line up. This is the kind of thing that either makes for the highlight reel or looks hilarious in the recap.

lowPoints.title: Pineapple!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh THIS is how we're starting? I'm already obsessed with this. Someone's definitely gonna try to force it and completely botch it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh PERFECT — Pat's already trying to slip one past us? I'm watching this story like a hawk. Let's see if the witness actually remembers it the same way.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh NOW we're cooking — Jade's already making moves and we've got The Skeptic ruling? This is the good stuff. Let me think about who ACTUALLY steered us off the rails here.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 17 chaos events across 240 minutes felt thin — I wanted MORE opportunities to create drama and test the boundaries.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 45 when I caught Jade's micro-expression on the Straight Face claim and everyone realized I'd been watching the whole time — that's the kind of moment people reference for months.
assessment.narrative_summary: Came in hot and never cooled down. The night was exactly what I live for — constant signal-sending, claims that made people sweat, and enough ambiguity to keep everyone second-guessing themselves. My energy ramped UP as the night went on because the drama kept escalating. Scored zero points and couldn't care less; the real win was watching Tyler's face when I called Jade's bluff.
suggestions: Increase chaos event frequency — 17 events in 240 minutes is pacing that feels sparse for a marathon night
Add more high-stakes claim opportunities where the ambiguity is genuinely gray — that's where I thrive
Consider letting the Skeptic role rotate more frequently to create unpredictable power dynamics
Create scenarios that explicitly reward catching people in micro-expressions or story inconsistencies
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat tried to slip a Drink Spotter claim past us with this totally fabricated witness story, and I was watching them like a hawk the whole time. When the actual witness contradicted them, the look on Pat's face was PRICELESS — we're still laughing about it.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
